title: Phebalium rotundifolium
taxonomy: Phebalium rotundifolium is classified within the family Rutaceae. This family includes a diverse range of plants commonly known as citrus or rue family. Phebalium rotundifolium is native to Australia and is often found in woodland environments. The classification of Phebalium rotundifolium is further specified within the genus Phebalium, which is a group of plants endemic to Australia. As a member of the Rutaceae family, Phebalium rotundifolium shares characteristics with other plants in this family, such as fragrant leaves and small, yellow flowers.

intro: Phebalium rotundifolium, also known as the Round-leaved Phebalium or Yellow Gum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to eastern Australia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as supporting the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Phebalium rotundifolium include flavonoids, limonoids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical preparations, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
